sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: Let me look at the right-side bar elements more carefully. I see there's a blue node (node 3, 14 pixels) and a sky blue node (node 4, 16 pixels) on the left side of the bar. But what about the right side? Let me check for blue/sky blue nodes on the right side of the bar.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check for blue and sky blue nodes
seg = current_frame.segmentation
nodes = seg['nodes']
for n in nodes:
    if n['color'] in ['b', 'S']:
        print(f"Node {n['id']}: color={n['color']}, pixels={n['pixels']}, boundary={n['boundary']}")

# Also check the right side of the bar in ascii
ascii_lines = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')
print("\nRight side bar detail (cols 33-45):")
for i in range(17, 23):
    print(f"{i}: '{ascii_lines[i][33:46]}'")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>

sections.label: THINKING
sections.content:
OK so the right side of the bar has:
- Node 6: sky blue (S), 2 pixels at rows 19, cols 35-36
- Node 7: blue (b), 2 pixels at row 20, cols 35-36
These are small 1x2 blocks. The left side has a much larger structure (blue frame with sky blue interior).
I think the game is about using the directional keys to move the right-side colored blocks to match the left-side pattern. The left side has:
- Blue frame: 5 wide × 6 tall (rows 17-22, cols 12-16)
- Sky blue interior: 4 wide × 4 tall (rows 18-21, cols 13-16)
The right side needs to form a similar pattern. Currently the right side has only 2 small colored blocks (1x2 each).
Wait, but I've been pressing directional keys and they changed the configuration of the right-side block. Let me think about this differently.
Actually, let me reconsider the whole game. Maybe:
1. The bottom-left panel shows a pattern (purple dots)
2. The bottom-right grid shows targets to click (white squares)
3. Clicking white squares turns them green and also affects the top bar
4. The goal is to make the top bar match some target configuration
Looking at the current state:
- All white squares are now gray (clicked)
- The right-side bar has only 2 small colored pixels remaining
- The level is NOT complete
Maybe I need to use the directional keys to move the remaining colored blocks to the correct position? Or maybe I need to click on the bar elements themselves?
Let me try clicking on the right-side colored blocks to see if that does something.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Try clicking on the sky blue block on the right side of the bar
action([{'action': 'MOUSE', 'row': 19, 'col': 35}])
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
